title: Data interpretation — CXR: tension pneumothorax
candidateInstructions: You are an FY1 in the resuscitation bay. Mr Owen Bradley, a 20-year-old man, has been brought by ambulance after a road traffic accident. He has become increasingly distressed with tachycardia HR 134, hypotension BP 82/50, tracheal deviation to the left and absent breath sounds on the right. A CXR has been taken. Please interpret the CXR and manage this patient.
examinerNotes: Tension pneumothorax is a CLINICAL DIAGNOSIS — do NOT wait for imaging. Candidate must recognise tension pneumothorax from clinical signs and perform needle decompression IMMEDIATELY. Critical error: waiting for CXR before treating, giving too much oxygen without treating the pneumothorax.

patient.name: Owen Bradley
patient.presentingComplaint: Worsening breathlessness and chest pain after RTA, hypotensive
patient.reasonForAttendance: Involved in road traffic accident, passenger, seatbelt injury

hpi.character: Sharp right chest pain, progressive breathlessness

systemsReview.respiratory: Absent breath sounds right, tracheal deviation to left

examination.general: Distressed young man, cyanotic, using accessory muscles
examination.cardiovascular: Tachycardia, hypotension — haemodynamically unstable, distended neck veins
examination.respiratory: Absent breath sounds right side. Hyper-resonance right side. Tracheal deviation to LEFT. Tachypnoeic.

investigations.name: Erect Chest X-ray
investigations.result: Large right-sided pneumothorax with complete collapse of right lung. Mediastinal shift to the LEFT. Tracheal deviation to left. Flattening of right hemidiaphragm. No rib fractures visible.
investigations.interpretation: TENSION PNEUMOTHORAX — RIGHT SIDE. THIS IS A CLINICAL DIAGNOSIS. DO NOT WAIT FOR CXR. Immediate needle decompression required: 2nd intercostal space, midclavicular line, right side. Follow with chest drain.

patient.emotionalState: Terrified, in extreme distress, struggling to breathe

dataGathering.criterion: Identifies tension pneumothorax from clinical signs
dataGathering.detailedExpectation: Tracheal deviation + absent breath sounds + hypotension = tension pneumo

dataGathering.criterion: States this is a CLINICAL DIAGNOSIS — does not wait for CXR
dataGathering.detailedExpectation: Proceeds to treatment based on clinical signs, not waiting for imaging

clinicalManagement.criterion: Chest drain insertion after needle decompression
clinicalManagement.detailedExpectation: Definitive treatment: chest drain 4th/5th ICS anterior axillary line
15L O2, IV access, fluids for hypotension

learningObjectives: Recognise tension pneumothorax as a clinical diagnosis
Perform needle decompression at correct site
Know definitive management with chest drain
